front: I'll give it to you tomorrow.
Are YOU (formal) giving it to me tomorrow?
Are YOU GUYS giving it to us tomorrow?
Are you guys giving me something?
I want to give it to you. (Te…)
hint: little words BEFORE the changed verb

front: I want to give it to you. (Quiero…)
hint: OR stuck on end of dar — accent on dár-

front: I want to give you something.
She doesn't give it to me.
Tomorrow I'll give it to you.

hint: subject at the end · order is flexible
reflexive — me is required, not optional
me = me / to me / for me — no extra word

front: He's speaking to me about it.
Shall I buy it for you?
Can you cook something for me?
I have cooked at six o'clock.

back: Me quedo aquí hasta las diez.

hint: subject for emphasis (me is still required)

front: Do you want to do it for me? (Quieres…)
hint: me + lo stuck on hacer · accent

front: my name is / I call myself

hint: reflexive stuck to end of -ar form

front: Are you planning to stay here tonight?

hint: no goes in front of everything

front: I want to know why you have to stay.
back: Quiero saber por qué tienes que quedarte.

front: Why don't you want to stay?
I want to buy a digital camera.
Do you see the big car?
I'm staying in the red car.
back: Me quedo en el auto rojo.

front: I have stayed in the red car.
back: Me he quedado en el auto rojo.

front: I have bought a red car.
I have bought myself a red car.
back: Me he comprado un auto rojo.
hint: me he comprado = bought for myself

front: Do you see the red car?
I can't find the red car.
back: No puedo encontrar el auto rojo.
